rationale_for_baseline_tier: Non-bullous impetigo without systemic toxicity is treated in the community with antibiotics (oral for extensive facial disease) and contagion control, with routine follow-up (T1). Cellulitis/sepsis carries a hard T4 safety-net; post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis routes to GP.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Deep, hot, spreading and very painful redness, high fever with rigors, or swelling around the eye
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — ED (000 if very unwell)
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests cellulitis/systemic infection needing urgent systemic treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness becomes deep, hot, very painful and spreading, you get a high fever with shivering, or your eye swells — go to the Emergency Department that day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Dark/cola-coloured urine or facial/leg swelling in the 1–3 weeks after the infection

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives with family — household transmission risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise contagion control and monitoring household contacts for lesions.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Take the antibiotics as prescribed, stop the steroid cream, gently wash off the crusts, and wash your hands and all towels/linen in hot water. You're contagious until 24 hours of antibiotics; it should stop spreading in about 2 days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Antibiotics + telehealth/GP follow-up; ED for cellulitis/sepsis; GP for dark urine later
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED for deep spreading painful redness or high fever at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Extensive non-bullous facial impetigo; cellulitis/eczema herpeticum screened negative; oral antibiotics; topical steroid stopped; PSGN safety-net given.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without antibiotics/contagion control or a cellulitis safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Impetigo progresses to facial cellulitis/sepsis, or spreads through the household.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Topical-only treatment for extensive facial disease, or omitting the PSGN safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for uncomplicated impetigo without systemic features — unnecessary.
